channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: AAUW International Project Grants 2026: Up to $10,000 for Women Leaders Advancing Gender Equity Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Women leaders committed to creating meaningful change in their communities now have an opportunity to secure funding through the AAUW International Project Grants 2026. Designed exclusively for AAUW International Fellowship Alumnae, this grant program supports community-based initiatives that promote gender equity and improve opportunities for women and girls around the world. With grants of up […]

The post AAUW International Project Grants 2026: Up to $10,000 for Women Leaders Advancing Gender Equity Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Women leaders committed to creating meaningful change in their communities now have an opportunity to secure funding through the AAUW International Project Grants 2026. Designed exclusively for AAUW International Fellowship Alumnae, this grant program supports community-based initiatives that promote gender equity and improve opportunities for women and girls around the world.

With grants of up to $10,000, the program empowers former AAUW International Fellows to apply their academic expertise, leadership skills, and professional experience to projects that generate direct and lasting impact in their home countries.

The initiative reflects AAUW’s long-standing commitment to advancing equity for women and girls through education, leadership development, and community engagement.

About the AAUW International Project Grants

The American Association of University Women (AAUW) established the International Project Grants program to help former International Fellowship recipients transform their academic knowledge into practical solutions that address gender inequality.

The program recognizes that many AAUW alumnae return to their home countries equipped with advanced education, professional expertise, and leadership experience. Through these grants, AAUW provides financial support that enables these women to launch, strengthen, or expand projects designed to improve the lives of women and girls.

The grants support initiatives that:

  • Advance gender equity
  • Empower women and girls
  • Promote educational opportunities
  • Strengthen communities
  • Address social and economic inequalities
  • Create sustainable local impact
  • Support leadership development among women

Unlike traditional academic funding, these grants focus on practical, community-based interventions that generate measurable outcomes.

Grant Value

Successful applicants may receive funding of up to:

💰 Award Ceiling: $10,000

The grant funds are intended to support approved project activities during the grant period.

Recipients must maintain all grant funds in an account dedicated specifically to project purposes.

Purpose of the Grant

The International Project Grants aim to help AAUW alumnae:

  • Apply academic knowledge to real-world challenges
  • Expand opportunities for women and girls
  • Lead impactful community initiatives
  • Strengthen gender equality efforts
  • Promote social and economic empowerment
  • Build sustainable programs within their communities

Projects should demonstrate a clear commitment to advancing the status of women and girls within the applicant’s home country.

Who Can Apply?

The AAUW International Project Grants are awarded exclusively to individuals rather than organizations.

To qualify, applicants must meet all eligibility requirements.

Applicants Must:

1. Be or Identify as a Woman

The program is specifically designed to support women leaders and changemakers.

2. Be an AAUW International Fellowship Alumna

Applicants must have previously received an AAUW International Fellowship.

3. Have Successfully Completed Their Fellowship Program

Applicants must have:

  • Completed the academic program funded through their AAUW International Fellowship
  • Fulfilled all reporting requirements associated with the fellowship

These requirements must be completed by June 30 of the year of application.

4. Reside in Their Home Country

Applicants must live in their home country throughout the grant period.

5. Implement the Project in Their Home Country

The proposed initiative must be carried out within the applicant’s home country.

6. Serve as the Primary Project Director

Applicants must maintain full responsibility for:

  • Programmatic decisions
  • Budget management
  • Administrative oversight
  • Editorial decisions
  • Project implementation

AAUW expects grant recipients to have direct control over all major aspects of the project.

Citizenship and Immigration Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be non-U.S. citizens, and
  • Not hold U.S. permanent resident status

The program is intended specifically for international alumnae who are implementing projects outside the United States.

What Types of Projects Are Supported?

AAUW International Project Grants support community-based initiatives that contribute to gender equality and women’s empowerment.

Examples may include:

Education Initiatives

Projects that:

  • Increase girls’ access to education
  • Improve literacy rates
  • Support STEM education for girls
  • Provide vocational training opportunities

Economic Empowerment Programs

Initiatives focused on:

  • Entrepreneurship
  • Financial literacy
  • Women’s employment opportunities
  • Income-generation projects

Leadership Development

Programs that:

  • Build leadership skills
  • Mentor emerging women leaders
  • Promote civic participation
  • Strengthen women’s representation

Health and Well-Being

Projects addressing:

  • Women’s health education
  • Maternal health awareness
  • Mental health support
  • Community wellness initiatives

Gender Equality Advocacy

Programs designed to:

  • Raise awareness of gender issues
  • Reduce discrimination
  • Promote women’s rights
  • Support policy engagement

Applicants should clearly demonstrate how their proposed project will create measurable benefits for women and girls.

Selection Criteria

AAUW evaluates proposals based on several key factors.

Strong applications typically demonstrate:

Clear Impact

Applicants should explain:

  • Who will benefit
  • How the project will create change
  • Expected outcomes and results

Community Relevance

Projects should address genuine needs within the local context.

Feasibility

AAUW looks for projects that can realistically be implemented within the grant period and budget.

Leadership Capacity

Applicants should demonstrate their ability to successfully manage and execute the project.

Sustainability

Projects should show potential for continued impact beyond the grant period.

Grant Timeline

Application Period

📅 September 16, 2024 – November 22, 2024

Applications must be submitted during this period.

Award Notification

📅 April 15, 2025

Applicants will be informed of funding decisions.

Grant Fund Disbursement

📅 July 2025

Funding may be distributed in full or in part.

Grant Period

📅 July 1, 2025 – June 30, 2026

All project activities should be implemented during this timeframe.

Interim Project Report

📅 December 15, 2025 (if applicable)

Some grantees may be required to submit a mid-project progress report.

Final Impact Report Deadline

📅 July 31, 2026

Grant recipients must submit a final report outlining project achievements, outcomes, and impact.

Important Funding Rules

Dedicated Project Account

Grant funds must be maintained in a dedicated account used solely for project purposes.

Approved Use of Funds

Funds may only be used:

  • For approved project activities
  • Within the approved grant period
  • According to the finalized grant proposal

No Retroactive Funding

Grant funds cannot be used for:

  • Expenses incurred before the grant start date
  • Expenses incurred after the grant end date

unless prior written approval is obtained from AAUW.

Legal Compliance

Grantees must comply with:

  • Local laws
  • National regulations
  • International legal requirements
  • Relevant financial compliance standards

Tax Considerations

AAUW does not:

  • Withhold U.S. taxes from grants
  • Provide tax forms to grantees
  • Offer tax advice

Recipients are responsible for determining whether any tax obligations exist in their home countries.

Applicants are encouraged to consult local tax professionals regarding grant-related tax requirements.

Why This Grant Matters

The AAUW International Project Grants represent more than financial support.

They provide former fellows with the opportunity to:

  • Translate academic knowledge into action
  • Address pressing gender challenges
  • Strengthen communities
  • Advance women’s rights
  • Create sustainable local impact
  • Expand leadership influence

By supporting alumnae-led initiatives, AAUW continues to invest in women leaders who are creating meaningful change worldwide.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should carefully review:

  • Eligibility requirements
  • Application guidelines
  • Required documentation
  • Grant policies

Applications must be submitted through AAUW’s official grant application process during the designated application period.

Prospective applicants are encouraged to prepare their proposals well in advance to ensure they meet all requirements.

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The AAUW International Project Grants 2026 provide an exceptional opportunity for AAUW International Fellowship Alumnae to leverage their education, expertise, and leadership experience to improve the lives of women and girls in their home countries.

With funding of up to $10,000, the program empowers women leaders to implement impactful, community-based projects that address gender inequality and foster sustainable development.

For eligible alumnae seeking to create lasting change, this grant offers both the resources and recognition needed to turn ideas into meaningful action.

For more information on this grant/fellowship visit here

For more global grant/fellowship opportunities on OFY, visit here

The post AAUW International Project Grants 2026: Up to $10,000 for Women Leaders Advancing Gender Equity Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Study, Research, and Build Global Networks in the United States
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026, one of the most respected international scholarship programs in the world. The scholarship provides Australian students, researchers, professionals, and scholars with the opportunity to undertake study, research, teaching, or professional development in the United States while building long-term academic, professional, and cultural connections. Established […]

The post Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Study, Research, and Build Global Networks in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026, one of the most respected international scholarship programs in the world. The scholarship provides Australian students, researchers, professionals, and scholars with the opportunity to undertake study, research, teaching, or professional development in the United States while building long-term academic, professional, and cultural connections.

Established to strengthen mutual understanding between Australia and the United States, the Fulbright Program has transformed the careers of thousands of scholars by providing access to leading universities, world-class research institutions, and influential professional networks.

Whether you are pursuing postgraduate studies, conducting advanced research, developing innovative solutions to global challenges, or expanding your professional expertise, the Fulbright Scholarship offers substantial financial support and a life-changing international experience.

About the Fulbright Scholarship Program

The Fulbright Program was established in Australia in 1949 through a binational treaty between the Australian and United States Governments, inspired by the vision of U.S. Senator J. William Fulbright.

Today, the program is administered by the Australian-American Fulbright Commission and remains one of the world’s most prestigious international exchange initiatives.

The mission of the Fulbright Program is to promote:

  • Educational exchange
  • Research collaboration
  • Cultural understanding
  • Academic excellence
  • Professional development
  • Long-term partnerships between Australia and the United States

Over the decades, Fulbright alumni have gone on to become leaders in government, academia, science, business, technology, healthcare, arts, and international affairs.

Why the Fulbright Scholarship Is So Prestigious

The Fulbright Scholarship is far more than a funding opportunity.

It is a globally recognized program that provides scholars with:

  • Access to leading U.S. universities
  • International research collaborations
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Leadership development
  • Cultural exchange experiences
  • Global recognition and credibility
  • Lifelong membership in the Fulbright alumni network

The program emphasizes not only academic achievement but also the ability of scholars to act as ambassadors who strengthen relationships between nations.

Scholarship Benefits

Fulbright Scholarships provide extensive financial and professional support.

Benefits vary depending on the specific award category and level of study, but generally include:

Monthly Living Stipend

Recipients receive a monthly stipend based on the cost of living in the city where their host institution is located.

This support helps cover:

  • Accommodation
  • Meals
  • Transportation
  • Daily living expenses

Tuition Support

Depending on the scholarship category, recipients may receive:

  • Partial tuition support for one academic year, or
  • Full tuition support under selected awards such as the Fulbright Future Award

Round-Trip International Airfare

The scholarship covers:

✈️ Economy-class return airfare

from the scholar’s home city in Australia to the host institution in the United States.

Establishment Allowance

Recipients receive financial assistance to help settle into their new academic environment upon arrival.

Dependent Allowance

Where applicable, additional support may be available for eligible dependents accompanying the scholar.

Placement Assistance

Graduate coursework applicants may receive assistance in identifying suitable U.S. host institutions through Fulbright Placement Services.

Comprehensive Health and Accident Insurance

The scholarship includes:

🏥 Health and Accident Insurance (ASPE)

Valued at up to:

USD $100,000

for the scholar.

Professional Networks

Scholars gain access to:

  • Fulbright alumni networks
  • Academic experts
  • International collaborators
  • Professional organizations
  • Research communities

Orientation and Enrichment Programs

Participants benefit from:

  • Pre-departure orientation in Australia
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Cultural enrichment activities
  • U.S.-based Fulbright events

Ongoing Support

The Fulbright Commission provides extensive support throughout the scholarship journey.

This includes:

  • Application guidance
  • Program coordination
  • Administrative support
  • Professional networking opportunities

Media and Publicity Opportunities

Recipients may also receive support in promoting their work and achievements through Fulbright communication channels.

Scholarship Duration

📅 One year or more

The duration varies depending on the award category and academic program.

Number of Scholarships Available

🎓 Approximately 10 scholarships are available.

Who Can Apply?

The Fulbright Scholarship supports a broad range of applicants.

Eligible categories include:

Postgraduate Students

Students pursuing:

  • Graduate coursework programs
  • Graduate research degrees

Postdoctoral Scholars

Researchers seeking advanced academic and research opportunities.

Professional Scholars

Professionals interested in developing expertise and strengthening international collaboration.

Senior Scholars

Experienced academics and researchers seeking to advance knowledge and international partnerships.

Eligible Study Levels

Applicants may apply under:

  • Graduate Coursework
  • Graduate Research

Eligible Applicant Types

Applications are open to:

  • Future students
  • Current students
  • Alumni

Fields of Study

One of the strengths of the Fulbright Program is its openness to all academic disciplines.

Eligible study areas include:

Agriculture and Veterinary Sciences

  • Agricultural innovation
  • Food systems
  • Animal health
  • Sustainability

Architecture, Building, Planning and Design

  • Urban development
  • Sustainable design
  • Smart cities

Arts, Humanities and Social Sciences

  • Cultural studies
  • Public policy
  • History
  • Sociology
  • International affairs

Business and Economics

  • Entrepreneurship
  • Finance
  • Economic development
  • Leadership

Education

  • Educational leadership
  • Learning innovation
  • Curriculum development

Engineering

  • Civil engineering
  • Mechanical engineering
  • Electrical engineering
  • Emerging technologies

Environment

  • Climate change
  • Environmental management
  • Sustainability

Health

  • Public health
  • Medical research
  • Health systems strengthening

Information Technology and Computer Science

  • Artificial intelligence
  • Cybersecurity
  • Data science
  • Software development

Law

  • International law
  • Human rights
  • Public policy

Music, Visual and Performing Arts

  • Creative practice
  • Arts management
  • Cultural exchange

Science

  • Natural sciences
  • Physical sciences
  • Applied sciences

Selection Criteria

Fulbright selection panels evaluate applicants based on several important attributes.

Academic and Professional Excellence

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Outstanding academic achievement
  • Strong professional accomplishments
  • Evidence of leadership potential

Strong Program Proposal

Successful candidates present:

  • Clear objectives
  • Well-developed project plans
  • Meaningful research or study goals

Potential Impact

Applicants should explain:

  • Expected outcomes
  • Long-term benefits
  • Broader societal contributions

Ambassadorial Qualities

The Fulbright Program values candidates who can:

  • Build international relationships
  • Represent Australia positively
  • Promote cross-cultural understanding
  • Foster collaboration between nations

Why You Should Apply

The Fulbright Scholarship is widely regarded as one of the most transformative international opportunities available to Australian scholars.

Benefits include:

✅ World-class education and research opportunities

✅ Fully funded support for study in the United States

✅ Access to prestigious academic institutions

✅ International professional networks

✅ Leadership development opportunities

✅ Enhanced career prospects

✅ Lifelong Fulbright alumni connections

For many recipients, the Fulbright experience becomes a defining milestone in their academic and professional journey.

Information Session

Prospective applicants can learn more through the official Fulbright information webinar.

Webinar Details

📅 Date: 12 May 2026

⏰ Time: 1:00 PM – 2:00 PM AEST

The session provides valuable insights into the application process, scholarship categories, and selection criteria.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted directly through the Fulbright Australia application system.

Applicants should carefully review all eligibility requirements and prepare their application materials well in advance of the deadline.

Apply Here 

Important Dates

Applications Open

📅 3 March 2026

Applications Close

📅 1 July 2026

Late applications are unlikely to be considered.

Final Thoughts

The Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026 offers a remarkable opportunity for Australian scholars to study, research, and collaborate in the United States while becoming part of one of the world’s most influential academic and professional networks.

With generous financial support, access to leading institutions, and unparalleled opportunities for international engagement, the Fulbright Program continues to empower future leaders who are committed to creating positive impact at local, national, and global levels.

If you aspire to expand your knowledge, strengthen international partnerships, and contribute meaningfully to your field, the Fulbright Scholarship may be the opportunity that transforms your future.

For more information on this scholarship visit here 

For more fully funded scholarships on OFY, visit here

The post Fulbright Scholarship Australia 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Study, Research, and Build Global Networks in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ISS Dakar Junior Fellow Opportunity 2026 in Senegal: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for Young Professionals in Peace and Security
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Institute for Security Studies (ISS) is inviting applications from talented young Senegalese researchers for its Junior Fellow position based in Dakar, Senegal. This prestigious 12-month fellowship offers a unique opportunity for early-career professionals to gain hands-on experience in peace and security research while working with one of Africa’s leading policy and research institutions. The […]

The post ISS Dakar Junior Fellow Opportunity 2026 in Senegal: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for Young Professionals in Peace and Security appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Institute for Security Studies (ISS) is inviting applications from talented young Senegalese researchers for its Junior Fellow position based in Dakar, Senegal. This prestigious 12-month fellowship offers a unique opportunity for early-career professionals to gain hands-on experience in peace and security research while working with one of Africa’s leading policy and research institutions.

The selected Junior Fellow will support the ISS Regional Office for West Africa and the Sahel by conducting research, contributing to policy analysis, organizing events, and monitoring political and security developments across the region. The fellowship provides an excellent platform for young researchers interested in governance, peacebuilding, conflict prevention, terrorism, violent extremism, and international affairs.

For aspiring policy analysts, researchers, and development professionals seeking to build a career in African peace and security studies, this opportunity offers valuable professional experience, mentorship, and exposure to regional and international policy discussions.

About the Institute for Security Studies (ISS)

Institute for Security Studies is one of Africa’s most respected independent research institutions dedicated to advancing human security, peace, governance, and sustainable development across the continent.

The organization works with governments, regional institutions, policymakers, civil society organizations, and international partners to:

  • Strengthen governance systems
  • Promote peace and stability
  • Prevent conflict and violence
  • Support evidence-based policymaking
  • Enhance regional security cooperation
  • Advance democratic institutions

Through its research, policy analysis, training programs, and stakeholder engagement initiatives, ISS contributes significantly to shaping policy responses to Africa’s most pressing security challenges.

About the Junior Fellow Position

The ISS Dakar Junior Fellow position is designed to provide practical research experience and professional development opportunities to young researchers interested in West African peace and security issues.

The successful candidate will join the ISS Regional Office for West Africa and the Sahel and work directly under the supervision of a Senior Researcher.

This fellowship combines:

  • Research experience
  • Policy analysis
  • Event coordination
  • Professional writing
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Regional security monitoring

The position offers a unique opportunity to contribute to meaningful policy discussions while developing critical analytical and research skills.

Fellowship Location

📍 Dakar, Senegal

The successful candidate will be based at the ISS Dakar office throughout the duration of the fellowship.

Dakar serves as an important political, diplomatic, and policy hub in West Africa, offering fellows exposure to regional institutions, international organizations, government actors, and development partners working across the region.

Fellowship Duration

Start Date

20 July 2026

Contract Length

12 Months

The selected fellow will work full-time during the fellowship period.

What Will the Junior Fellow Do?

The Junior Fellow will support the ISS research team across a range of activities.

1. Conduct Background Research

The fellow will:

  • Gather information on regional security developments
  • Review relevant literature and policy documents
  • Support ongoing research projects
  • Assist in data collection and analysis

2. Undertake Desk and Field Research

Under the guidance of a Senior Researcher, the fellow will:

  • Conduct independent research
  • Collect qualitative and quantitative information
  • Participate in field missions where required
  • Contribute to policy-relevant research outputs

3. Support Event Organization

The fellow will help organize:

  • Workshops
  • Policy dialogues
  • Seminars
  • Conferences
  • Stakeholder consultations

These events may take place in Dakar and selected countries across the region.

4. Attend Conferences and Seminars

Responsibilities include:

  • Participating in policy discussions
  • Taking detailed notes
  • Preparing event reports
  • Summarizing key findings and recommendations

5. Prepare Meeting Documentation

The fellow will:

  • Record meeting discussions
  • Draft meeting minutes
  • Support documentation processes
  • Maintain organized records

6. Monitor Political and Security Developments

An important responsibility includes:

  • Tracking developments across West Africa and the Sahel
  • Preparing weekly political and security reviews
  • Presenting findings to the research team
  • Identifying emerging trends and risks

7. Write Policy Articles

The selected candidate will:

  • Draft short analytical articles
  • Contribute to ISS publications
  • Develop policy-focused content
  • Improve research communication skills

All writing activities will be conducted under the supervision of a Senior Researcher.

8. Support Additional Project Activities

The fellow may also undertake other project-related tasks assigned by the Senior Researcher.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for:

  • Recent master’s graduates
  • Early-career researchers
  • Policy analysts
  • International relations graduates
  • Social science researchers
  • Governance specialists
  • Peacebuilding practitioners
  • Security studies enthusiasts

Applicants should have a strong interest in:

  • Peace and security
  • Governance
  • Conflict prevention
  • Terrorism studies
  • Violent extremism
  • Regional cooperation
  • Political analysis

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for this fellowship, applicants must meet the following criteria.

Nationality Requirement

Applicants must be:

✅ Nationals of Senegal

This opportunity is not open to applicants from other countries.

Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

✅ Under 30 years of age

Educational Requirement

Applicants must hold a:

✅ Master’s Degree

in one of the following fields:

  • Anthropology
  • Sociology
  • Politics
  • Law
  • History
  • International Relations
  • Related disciplines

Language Requirements

Applicants must be fluent in:

Either

  • English

or

  • French

Both written and spoken proficiency are required.

Additional Advantage

Knowledge of one or more local languages will be considered an asset.

Required Skills and Competencies

Successful candidates should demonstrate:

Research Skills

  • Literature review
  • Data collection
  • Policy analysis
  • Report writing
  • Critical thinking

Organizational Skills

  • Time management
  • Planning and coordination
  • Attention to detail
  • Reliability

Communication Skills

  • Strong writing abilities
  • Presentation skills
  • Professional communication
  • Team collaboration

Technical Skills

Applicants should be proficient in:

  • Microsoft Word
  • Microsoft Excel
  • Microsoft PowerPoint
  • Internet research tools
  • Prezi

Personal Qualities

The ISS seeks candidates who are:

  • Self-motivated
  • Curious
  • Adaptable
  • Professional
  • Responsible
  • Able to work under pressure

Fellowship Benefits

Monthly Stipend

The selected Junior Fellow will receive a monthly stipend throughout the fellowship period.

Professional Development

Participants will gain:

  • Practical policy research experience
  • Exposure to regional security issues
  • Mentorship from senior researchers
  • Publication opportunities
  • Networking opportunities
  • Event management experience

Career Advancement

The fellowship provides valuable experience for careers in:

  • Research institutions
  • International organizations
  • Government agencies
  • Think tanks
  • Development organizations
  • Academic institutions

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

The Institute for Security Studies is committed to promoting employment equity and inclusive participation.

The organization particularly encourages:

👩 Female applicants to apply.

ISS values diversity and seeks to create an inclusive work environment where individuals from different backgrounds can contribute meaningfully to its mission.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their application via email.

Application Email

dakarjobs@issafrica.org

Contact Person

Souleymane Ndiaye

Apply Here 

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

1. Cover Letter

Clearly explaining:

  • Motivation for applying
  • Relevant experience
  • Interest in peace and security research

2. Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Including:

  • Educational background
  • Professional experience
  • Research experience
  • Skills and competencies

3. Referees

Provide at least:

  • Three contactable referees

4. Identity Document

A copy of a valid identity document must be included.

Selection Process

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further stages of the recruitment process.

Applicants are encouraged to submit complete and well-prepared applications before the deadline.

Application Deadline

19 June 2026

Late submissions may not be considered.

Why You Should Apply

The ISS Dakar Junior Fellow opportunity offers a rare chance for young Senegalese professionals to build a strong foundation in policy research and security analysis while contributing to important conversations shaping the future of West Africa and the Sahel.

By joining ISS, fellows will gain practical experience, professional mentorship, and exposure to regional policy processes that can significantly strengthen their future academic and professional careers.

For young researchers passionate about peacebuilding, governance, security, and public policy, this fellowship represents an outstanding stepping stone into Africa’s policy and research landscape.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post ISS Dakar Junior Fellow Opportunity 2026 in Senegal: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for Young Professionals in Peace and Security appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Northern Illinois University Political Science Graduate Programs 2026: Fully Funded Master’s and Doctoral Opportunities in the United States
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Students interested in advancing their academic and professional careers in political science now have an excellent opportunity to apply for graduate studies at Northern Illinois University (NIU). Through its Department of Political Science within the College of Liberal Arts and Sciences, NIU offers Master of Arts (M.A.) and Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) programs designed to […]

The post Northern Illinois University Political Science Graduate Programs 2026: Fully Funded Master’s and Doctoral Opportunities in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students interested in advancing their academic and professional careers in political science now have an excellent opportunity to apply for graduate studies at Northern Illinois University (NIU). Through its Department of Political Science within the College of Liberal Arts and Sciences, NIU offers Master of Arts (M.A.) and Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) programs designed to prepare students for leadership roles in academia, government, public policy, international affairs, law, research, and many other professional fields.

The university provides a supportive academic environment, distinguished faculty mentorship, and competitive funding opportunities for qualified graduate students. Applicants can pursue specialized studies across several major fields of political science while benefiting from assistantships, fellowships, tuition waivers, and stipends.

Application Deadline

January 15

While applications may sometimes be considered after the deadline, prospective students are strongly encouraged to apply before January 15. Applicants wishing to submit materials after the deadline should contact the department directly before applying.

Why Study Political Science at Northern Illinois University?

Northern Illinois University’s Department of Political Science offers an advanced educational experience that equips students with the analytical, research, and professional skills needed to excel in a wide range of careers.

The department consists of:

  • Approximately 12 distinguished faculty members.
  • Around 45 graduate students.
  • A collaborative and supportive academic community.
  • Opportunities for research, teaching, and professional development.

Graduate students work closely with faculty mentors and fellow scholars while pursuing their academic and professional goals.

Areas of Specialization

Students can tailor their graduate studies based on their interests by selecting one of the department’s major areas of study.

1. American Government and Public Law

This concentration focuses on:

  • American political institutions.
  • Constitutional law.
  • Public policy.
  • Judicial processes.
  • Political behavior.
  • Governance and administration.

2. Comparative Politics

Students explore:

  • Political systems around the world.
  • Democratization and political development.
  • Comparative institutions.
  • Political transitions.
  • Governance across different countries and regions.

3. International Relations

This specialization examines:

  • Global politics.
  • International security.
  • Foreign policy.
  • International organizations.
  • Diplomacy.
  • Global governance.

4. Political Theory

Students engage with:

  • Classical political thought.
  • Contemporary political philosophy.
  • Democratic theory.
  • Ethics and governance.
  • Normative approaches to political analysis.

Graduate Degree Options

Master of Arts (M.A.) in Political Science

The M.A. program is designed for students seeking advanced political science training and can serve as preparation for:

  • Doctoral studies.
  • Public service careers.
  • Policy analysis.
  • Government positions.
  • Research roles.
  • Nonprofit and international organizations.

Expected Completion Time

Students can typically complete the Master of Arts degree within:

  • Two years.
  • Four semesters of full-time study.

Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) in Political Science

The Ph.D. program provides advanced scholarly training for students interested in academic, research, and leadership careers.

Completion Timeline for Students with a Master’s Degree

Students entering with an existing M.A. can typically complete the Ph.D. within:

  • Five to six semesters.

Completion Timeline for Students without a Master’s Degree

Students entering directly into the doctoral pathway can generally complete the program within:

  • Five years.
  • Ten semesters.

Students may complete Master’s degree requirements while progressing toward the Ph.D.

Graduate Funding Opportunities

One of the most attractive features of the Northern Illinois University Political Science graduate program is the availability of funding support for qualified students.

Funding is awarded competitively and is available to applicants who maintain strong academic standing.

The department also collaborates with other university units, including specialized centers and programs, to help secure additional funding opportunities.

Available funding may include:

  • Graduate assistantships.
  • Fellowships.
  • Tuition waivers.
  • Research support opportunities.

Graduate Assistantship Expectations

Students who receive assistantships and tuition waivers are generally expected to:

  • Enroll full time.
  • Work with graduate faculty members.
  • Perform assigned academic duties.
  • Work no more than 20 hours per week.

These assistantships provide valuable professional experience while helping students finance their education.

Funding for Master’s Degree Students

Students pursuing the Master of Arts degree can typically expect:

  • Up to two years of graduate assistantship support.
  • Financial assistance during fall and spring semesters.
  • A graduate stipend.
  • A tuition waiver covering most tuition-related expenses.

This funding significantly reduces educational costs while allowing students to focus on their academic goals.

Funding for Doctoral Students

Doctoral students may receive:

  • Up to five years of funding support.
  • Graduate assistantships.
  • Tuition-related support.
  • Professional development opportunities.

The department indicates that the minimum Ph.D. assistantship award is approximately:

$14,000 per academic year

Applicants may contact the department for the most current stipend information.

Career Opportunities After Graduation

Graduates of Northern Illinois University’s Political Science programs have successfully established careers across numerous disciplines and sectors.

Potential career paths include:

  • University teaching and research.
  • Government administration.
  • Public policy analysis.
  • International affairs.
  • Diplomacy.
  • Public law and legal professions.
  • Political consulting.
  • Nonprofit leadership.
  • Public service organizations.
  • Think tanks and research institutions.

The program’s broad academic training enables graduates to pursue diverse professional opportunities both within and beyond academia.

Supportive Academic Community

Graduate students benefit from:

  • Close faculty mentorship.
  • Research collaboration opportunities.
  • Professional networking.
  • Interdisciplinary engagement.
  • Academic conferences and scholarly activities.
  • A welcoming graduate student community.

The department’s relatively small size allows students to receive personalized attention and guidance throughout their studies.

How to Apply

Prospective students should submit an application for admission to the Northern Illinois University Political Science graduate program.

Applicants are encouraged to:

  1. Review graduate admission requirements.
  2. Prepare application materials early.
  3. Submit applications before January 15.
  4. Explore available funding opportunities.
  5. Contact the department with questions regarding admissions or assistantships.

Early application is highly recommended due to the competitive nature of funding awards.

APPLY HERE

Final Thoughts

Northern Illinois University’s Political Science graduate programs offer an excellent opportunity for students seeking advanced education, specialized training, and financial support in the field of political science. With strong faculty mentorship, multiple areas of specialization, generous funding opportunities, and pathways to diverse career outcomes, the program provides a solid foundation for future scholars, policymakers, researchers, and public service professionals.

Students interested in pursuing a fully funded graduate education in the United States should strongly consider applying before the January 15 deadline to maximize their chances of admission and funding consideration.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Northern Illinois University Political Science Graduate Programs 2026: Fully Funded Master’s and Doctoral Opportunities in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026: Apply to Become an AUDA-NEPAD Youth SRH Ambassador
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Key Details Opportunity African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026 Host Organization AUDA-NEPAD (African Union Development Agency) Eligible Applicants Young Africans and members of the African diaspora Age Requirement 18–35 years Application Deadline 17 July 2026 Opportunity Type Youth Ambassador Programme (Voluntary) Focus Areas Sexual and Reproductive Health (SRH), Youth Leadership, Advocacy, Community Mobilization, Digital […]

The post African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026: Apply to Become an AUDA-NEPAD Youth SRH Ambassador appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Key Details

Opportunity African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026
Host Organization AUDA-NEPAD (African Union Development Agency)
Eligible Applicants Young Africans and members of the African diaspora
Age Requirement 18–35 years
Application Deadline 17 July 2026
Opportunity Type Youth Ambassador Programme (Voluntary)
Focus Areas Sexual and Reproductive Health (SRH), Youth Leadership, Advocacy, Community Mobilization, Digital Engagement, Youth Empowerment
Benefits Recognition, training, mentorship, networking, advocacy opportunities, visibility, and sponsored participation in selected engagements
Funding No financial remuneration; selected ambassadors receive leadership development, exposure, and participation opportunities
Application Method Online Application Form

Applications Open for the African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative

Are you a passionate young African leader committed to advancing Sexual and Reproductive Health (SRH), youth empowerment, and sustainable development? The African Union Development Agency (AUDA-NEPAD) is inviting applications for the African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026, a prestigious continental opportunity designed to empower young Africans to champion health, wellbeing, and youth leadership across the continent.

The application deadline is 17 July 2026.

About the AUDA-NEPAD Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative

Africa is home to the largest youth population in history, with more than 400 million young people aged 15–35. By 2050, nearly 40% of the world’s youth will live in Africa. Harnessing this demographic advantage requires strategic investments in youth development, leadership, governance, and health.

Recognizing the critical role of Sexual and Reproductive Health (SRH) in Africa’s development, AUDA-NEPAD has launched the Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative to place young people at the center of efforts to achieve the continent’s development goals.

The initiative is guided by the Energize Africa Framework, which views young people as active partners and co-creators of change rather than passive beneficiaries. Through advocacy, awareness creation, and community engagement, selected ambassadors will help amplify youth voices and contribute to a healthier, more prosperous Africa.

Why Sexual and Reproductive Health Matters for Africa’s Future

Sexual and Reproductive Health is more than a health issue—it is a key driver of economic growth, social development, and youth empowerment. Strengthening SRH outcomes is essential for realizing Africa’s Demographic Dividend and advancing the aspirations of Agenda 2063, particularly Goal 18: “Engaged and Empowered Youth and Children.”

By connecting SRH advocacy with youth leadership, AUDA-NEPAD seeks to empower a new generation of changemakers who can influence policies, educate communities, and drive meaningful transformation across the continent.

Roles and Responsibilities of Youth SRH Ambassadors

Selected ambassadors will:

  • Promote youth participation in Sexual and Reproductive Health advocacy.
  • Amplify AUDA-NEPAD flagship programmes, including Energize Africa and A2DSRH.
  • Raise awareness of key African Union frameworks and instruments.
  • Advocate for youth health, wellbeing, and empowerment.
  • Bridge grassroots realities with national, regional, and continental priorities.
  • Engage communities through campaigns, digital advocacy, and youth mobilization efforts.
  • Ensure youth perspectives contribute to shaping Africa’s future development agenda.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be young Africans aged 18–35 years, including members of the African diaspora.
  • Demonstrate a strong commitment to Sexual and Reproductive Health advocacy and youth empowerment.
  • Have an active digital presence and experience in social media engagement, content creation, or advocacy campaigns.
  • Show the ability to mobilize peers and communities.
  • Represent diverse regions, genders, languages, and marginalized groups across Africa.

Added Advantage

Knowledge of:

  • Agenda 2063
  • The African Youth Charter
  • The Maputo Protocol
  • The African Union 2017 Demographic Dividend Roadmap

will strengthen an application.

Benefits of Becoming an AUDA-NEPAD Youth SRH Ambassador

Successful applicants will receive:

Continental Recognition

Gain official recognition as an AUDA-NEPAD Youth SRH Ambassador.

Advocacy Opportunities

Contribute to important discussions on youth health, wellbeing, and development at regional, continental, and global levels.

Enhanced Visibility

Increase your profile through participation in high-level youth and SRH platforms across Africa.

Training and Mentorship

Access capacity-building opportunities, leadership development programmes, mentorship, and empowerment initiatives.

Networking Opportunities

Connect with policymakers, development practitioners, youth leaders, and influential networks across the continent.

Influence Africa’s Development Agenda

Contribute directly to advancing Africa’s Demographic Dividend and youth empowerment priorities.

Sponsored Participation

Selected ambassadors may receive sponsored opportunities to participate in AUDA-NEPAD youth and SRH-related engagements and events.

Important Note

The Youth SRH Ambassador role is voluntary and does not provide financial remuneration. However, it offers exceptional opportunities for leadership development, professional visibility, advocacy experience, networking, and meaningful contribution to Africa’s youth and health agenda.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official AUDA-NEPAD online application platform.

Application Deadline

📅 17 July 2026

Young leaders who are passionate about Sexual and Reproductive Health, youth empowerment, leadership, and Africa’s development are strongly encouraged to apply and become part of a growing movement shaping the future of the continent.

Final Thoughts

The African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for young Africans to lead change, influence policy discussions, advocate for youth wellbeing, and contribute to achieving Africa’s development aspirations. If you are passionate about SRH advocacy, youth leadership, and creating lasting impact in your community and beyond, this programme could be the perfect platform to amplify your voice and shape Africa’s future.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official AUDA-NEPAD online application platform.

The post African Union Youth SRH Ambassadors Initiative 2026: Apply to Become an AUDA-NEPAD Youth SRH Ambassador appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ECOSOCC Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme 2026 in Zambia: Fully Funded Professional Development Opportunity for Young Zambian Graduates
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Economic, Social and Cultural Council (ECOSOCC) of the African Union has officially opened applications for the prestigious Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme 2026, a unique career development opportunity designed to equip young Zambian graduates with practical experience in international governance, policy research, diplomacy, project management, and stakeholder engagement. Named in honor of Zambia’s founding […]

The post ECOSOCC Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme 2026 in Zambia: Fully Funded Professional Development Opportunity for Young Zambian Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Economic, Social and Cultural Council (ECOSOCC) of the African Union has officially opened applications for the prestigious Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme 2026, a unique career development opportunity designed to equip young Zambian graduates with practical experience in international governance, policy research, diplomacy, project management, and stakeholder engagement.

Named in honor of Zambia’s founding President and renowned Pan-African statesman, Kenneth Kaunda, this highly competitive programme offers selected graduates the opportunity to work directly within the ECOSOCC Secretariat in Lusaka, Zambia, for a period of nine months. Participants will gain first-hand experience of how an African Union institution operates while contributing to meaningful projects that support Africa’s development agenda.

The programme forms part of the ECOSOCC University Outreach Initiative and is implemented in collaboration with universities and research institutions across Zambia. It aims to nurture the next generation of African professionals while strengthening youth participation in continental governance and policy processes.

About the Economic, Social and Cultural Council (ECOSOCC)

The Economic, Social and Cultural Council (ECOSOCC) is the advisory organ of the African Union responsible for promoting and facilitating the active participation of African civil society organizations in the development and implementation of policies and programmes across the continent.

Established in 2004 under Articles 5(i) and 22 of the African Union Constitutive Act, ECOSOCC serves as a bridge between the African Union and civil society organizations operating throughout Africa and the African Diaspora.

Through its initiatives and programmes, ECOSOCC seeks to ensure that citizens, youth, academia, and civil society stakeholders contribute meaningfully to shaping Africa’s future.

What is the Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme?

The Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme is a professional training and work-placement initiative that provides recent graduates with direct exposure to the operations of an African Union institution.

Participants are embedded within the ECOSOCC Secretariat, where they actively contribute to organizational activities and strategic initiatives while developing valuable professional competencies.

Rather than serving as observers, trainees are entrusted with real responsibilities and are expected to contribute meaningfully to the Secretariat’s daily operations and long-term objectives.

The programme seeks to:

  • Build a pool of highly skilled Zambian professionals familiar with African Union systems and processes.
  • Strengthen youth engagement in governance and development.
  • Enhance the employability and leadership potential of young graduates.
  • Promote knowledge sharing between academia and continental institutions.
  • Develop future policy experts, researchers, diplomats, and development practitioners.

Areas of Work and Learning

Selected participants will be involved in a variety of professional assignments and activities, including:

1. Policy Research

Trainees will support evidence-based policy development through:

  • Conducting research and analysis.
  • Reviewing policy documents.
  • Preparing reports and briefing papers.
  • Supporting policy formulation initiatives.

2. Project Coordination

Participants will gain hands-on experience in:

  • Programme planning.
  • Project implementation.
  • Monitoring and evaluation.
  • Administrative coordination.
  • Event management.

3. Stakeholder Engagement

Trainees will work closely with various stakeholders including:

  • Civil society organizations.
  • Government institutions.
  • Academic institutions.
  • Development partners.
  • African Union bodies.

4. Secretariat Programme Support

Participants will contribute to:

  • Organizational planning.
  • Strategic initiatives.
  • Communication activities.
  • Research projects.
  • Continental engagement programmes.

Benefits of the ECOSOCC Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme 2026

The programme offers extensive professional and career development benefits, including:

Practical Professional Experience

Participants gain valuable hands-on experience in:

  • International governance.
  • Policy development.
  • Programme implementation.
  • Research and analysis.
  • Institutional operations.

Specialized Training

Selected graduates will receive training in:

  • Diplomacy.
  • Research methodologies.
  • Advocacy.
  • Project management.
  • Policy processes.

Mentorship Opportunities

Participants will benefit from mentorship and guidance provided by:

  • Senior ECOSOCC personnel.
  • African Union experts.
  • Experienced professionals in governance and development.

Exposure to High-Level Meetings

Trainees may participate in:

  • African Union meetings.
  • Policy dialogues.
  • International conferences.
  • Stakeholder consultations.
  • Regional and continental engagements.

Professional Networking

The programme provides access to a broad professional network across:

  • African Union institutions.
  • Pan-African organizations.
  • Academic institutions.
  • Civil society organizations.
  • Development partners.

Official Recommendation

Participants who successfully complete the programme will receive an official recommendation that can significantly strengthen future academic and professional applications.

Monthly Transport Stipend

Although the programme does not provide a salary, successful participants receive:

  • A monthly transport stipend.
  • Logistical support from ECOSOCC.
  • Transportation assistance to and from the Secretariat office.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements:

  • Be a Zambian national.
  • Be under 35 years of age at the time of application.
  • Hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant discipline.
  • Have obtained their degree within the last five years.
  • Have graduated with Distinction or Merit.
  • Demonstrate strong analytical skills.
  • Demonstrate excellent research abilities.
  • Possess strong writing and communication skills.
  • Be fluent in English.

Additional language proficiency in French or another African Union working language will be considered an advantage.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following documents:

1. Letter of Motivation

The motivation letter should:

  • Be written in English.
  • Not exceed one page.
  • Use font size 12.
  • Explain interest in the programme.
  • Outline career aspirations.
  • Demonstrate suitability for the programme.

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Applicants should provide a current and detailed CV highlighting academic achievements, skills, and relevant experiences.

3. Certified Degree Certificates

Applicants must submit university-certified copies of all relevant degree certificates.

4. Certified Academic Transcripts

Official university-certified transcripts must be included.

5. Recommendation Letters

Applicants must provide:

  • Two signed recommendation letters.

Programme Duration and Location

Duration

  • Nine months.
  • May be extended up to one year.

Duty Station

ECOSOCC Secretariat

Lusaka, Zambia

Programme Commencement Date

  • 1 August 2026

Supervision and Performance Assessment

Participants will:

  • Report directly to the Head of Programmes at ECOSOCC.
  • Participate in regular performance reviews.
  • Submit progress reports throughout the programme period.

Selection Process

The selection process consists of two stages:

Stage One: Written Assessment

Applicants will complete a written assessment evaluating their competencies and suitability.

Stage Two: Interview

Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews conducted:

  • In person, or
  • Via video conference.

Candidates must present original academic certificates and supporting documents during the verification process.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: 26 June 2026 at 23:59 Central African Time (CAT).

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Successful candidates will be notified after the interview process.

How to Apply

Interested and eligible applicants should submit their applications before the deadline through the official ECOSOCC application portal.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare all required documents early and ensure that submissions are complete and accurate.

APPLY HERE

Enquiries

All enquiries regarding the programme should be directed to the ECOSOCC Secretariat through:

Email: recruitment@ecosocc.au.int

Address:
ECOSOCC Secretariat
8658 Kudu Road
Kabulonga
Lusaka, Zambia

This graduate immersion programme represents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious young Zambian graduates seeking practical experience within the African Union system while building careers in governance, policy, diplomacy, development, and international cooperation.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Internship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post ECOSOCC Kenneth Kaunda Graduate Immersion Programme 2026 in Zambia: Fully Funded Professional Development Opportunity for Young Zambian Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations Population Fund Youth, Peace and Security Internship 2026 in Dakar, Senegal (Stipends are available)
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) has opened applications for its prestigious Youth, Peace and Security Internship Programme 2026 at the West and Central Africa Regional Office (WCARO) in Dakar, Senegal. This six-month internship offers an exceptional opportunity for students, recent graduates, and young professionals who are passionate about peacebuilding, youth engagement, humanitarian action, conflict […]

The post United Nations Population Fund Youth, Peace and Security Internship 2026 in Dakar, Senegal (Stipends are available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) has opened applications for its prestigious Youth, Peace and Security Internship Programme 2026 at the West and Central Africa Regional Office (WCARO) in Dakar, Senegal. This six-month internship offers an exceptional opportunity for students, recent graduates, and young professionals who are passionate about peacebuilding, youth engagement, humanitarian action, conflict prevention, and sustainable development.

Hosted by the United Nations agency responsible for advancing sexual and reproductive health, gender equality, and youth empowerment worldwide, this internship provides hands-on experience within a multicultural and international work environment. Selected interns will work closely with the Youth, Peace and Security (YPS) Specialist and contribute to impactful regional initiatives that support young people’s participation in peacebuilding and security processes across West and Central Africa.

Application Deadline: 19 June 2026

About the United Nations Population Fund

UNFPA is the lead United Nations agency working to ensure that every pregnancy is wanted, every childbirth is safe, and every young person’s potential is fulfilled. Through its Strategic Plan, UNFPA focuses on three transformative goals:

  • Ending preventable maternal deaths
  • Ending unmet need for family planning
  • Ending gender-based violence and harmful practices

The organization works closely with governments, civil society organizations, youth groups, and international partners to advance human rights, gender equality, and sustainable development.

The Youth, Peace and Security Internship aligns with these priorities by strengthening youth participation in peacebuilding, mediation, conflict prevention, and humanitarian action.

Internship Overview

Position Details

  • Position Title: Youth, Peace and Security Intern
  • Organization: United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA)
  • Regional Office: West and Central Africa Regional Office (WCARO)
  • Location: Dakar, Senegal
  • Internship Duration: 6 Months
  • Employment Type: Full-Time Internship
  • Internship Modality: In-Person (Remote arrangements may be discussed)
  • Vacancy Number: 34675
  • Required Language: French
  • Desired Language: English
  • Additional Advantage: Portuguese and/or Spanish
  • Application Deadline: 19 June 2026

The selected intern will work under the direct supervision of the Youth, Peace and Security Specialist while collaborating with multiple programme teams across the regional office.

A structured work plan will be developed to ensure meaningful learning opportunities and measurable achievements throughout the internship period.

Key Responsibilities

Interns will contribute to several strategic areas of the Youth, Peace and Security agenda.

1. Research and Analysis

Successful candidates will support research and evidence generation activities related to youth participation in peacebuilding and security initiatives.

Responsibilities include:

  • Conducting background research on youth participation in peacebuilding, conflict resolution, and humanitarian action
  • Monitoring national and regional Youth, Peace and Security policies
  • Compiling reports and policy updates
  • Supporting the preparation of briefing notes
  • Assisting with fact sheets and policy documents
  • Contributing to knowledge products related to Youth, Peace and Security themes

2. Project Support

Interns will assist in the implementation and coordination of regional programmes and initiatives.

Key activities include:

  • Supporting regional planning and coordination of Youth, Peace and Security projects
  • Assisting country offices with project implementation activities
  • Supporting youth engagement initiatives in peace processes
  • Contributing to mediation and conflict-prevention programmes
  • Assisting with the design of youth-led peacebuilding initiatives
  • Supporting the delivery of training sessions and capacity-building activities

3. Communication and Advocacy

The internship also provides valuable communications experience.

Tasks include:

  • Drafting social media content
  • Preparing newsletters and blog articles
  • Developing advocacy materials for youth-focused campaigns
  • Supporting public awareness initiatives related to peace and security
  • Assisting in organizing webinars and virtual dialogues
  • Supporting workshops involving young peacebuilders and youth organizations

4. Partnership and Coordination

Interns will gain exposure to stakeholder engagement and partnership management.

Activities include:

  • Supporting collaboration with youth networks
  • Engaging civil society organizations and strategic partners
  • Documenting best practices and lessons learned
  • Participating in inter-agency coordination meetings
  • Providing administrative support for Youth, Peace and Security coordination efforts

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for this internship, applicants must meet UNFPA’s educational requirements and demonstrate a strong interest in development and peacebuilding.

Educational Requirements

Applicants must satisfy at least one of the following conditions:

Option A

  • Be enrolled in a bachelor’s degree programme or equivalent
  • Have completed at least one full academic year

Option B

  • Be enrolled in a postgraduate degree programme such as a master’s degree or higher

Option C

  • Have recently graduated from a university programme
  • Begin the internship within one year of graduation if selected

Option D

  • Be enrolled in a postgraduate professional trainee programme
  • Complete the internship as part of the programme requirements

Required Skills and Qualifications

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong interest in development, peace and security, and youth engagement
  • Excellent research and analytical skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
  • Ability to work independently
  • Strong teamwork and collaboration skills
  • Good communication abilities
  • Adaptability and cultural sensitivity
  • Capacity to work effectively in multicultural environments

Preferred Qualifications

The following experiences are considered advantageous:

  • Volunteering experience in youth-led initiatives
  • Experience in peacebuilding programmes
  • Humanitarian work experience
  • Engagement in community development activities
  • Participation in youth advocacy projects

Language Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate:

Required

  • Written and spoken proficiency in French
  • Written and spoken proficiency in English

Additional Assets

  • Portuguese language skills
  • Spanish language skills

Candidates with multilingual abilities may have an added advantage in the selection process.

Learning Opportunities and Professional Development

One of the most attractive aspects of this internship is the extensive learning experience provided by UNFPA.

Upon completion of the programme, interns will gain:

Institutional Knowledge

  • Understanding of the UNFPA mandate
  • Knowledge of regional office structures and operations
  • Familiarity with United Nations systems and procedures

Technical Expertise

  • Deep understanding of the Youth, Peace and Security agenda
  • Knowledge of programme design and implementation
  • Exposure to development and humanitarian programming

Professional Skills

  • Experience drafting substantive policy and programme documents
  • Exposure to UN technical language and reporting standards
  • Understanding of coordination mechanisms within the UN system
  • Practical communication and advocacy experience

Networking Opportunities

Interns will have opportunities to:

  • Engage with UN professionals
  • Build professional networks
  • Collaborate with experts across multiple sectors
  • Gain international work experience

Multicultural Work Experience

Participants will:

  • Work within diverse international teams
  • Strengthen cross-cultural communication skills
  • Develop global professional competencies
  • Build leadership and collaboration capacities

Financial Support and Stipend Information

UNFPA internships are unpaid positions; however, eligible interns may receive a stipend.

Important details include:

  • A stipend may be provided to help cover basic daily expenses
  • Stipends are available for interns who are not financially supported by another institution, scholarship, foundation, university, or government programme
  • Stipend amounts vary depending on the agreed duty station and work arrangement

Medical Insurance Requirement

Applicants should note that:

  • Medical insurance is mandatory
  • Proof of insurance must be submitted before the internship begins
  • UNFPA does not provide medical insurance coverage for interns

Core Competencies Sought by UNFPA

Successful candidates are expected to demonstrate:

Values

  • Integrity
  • Commitment to UNFPA and the United Nations system
  • Respect for diversity
  • Openness to change

Core Competencies

  • Results orientation
  • Accountability
  • Professional expertise
  • Strategic thinking
  • Teamwork and relationship management

Functional Competencies

  • Analytical skills
  • Communication skills
  • Teamwork
  • Client orientation

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Commitment

UNFPA is committed to creating an inclusive workplace that values diversity and equal opportunity.

The organization particularly encourages applications from:

  • Women
  • Persons with disabilities
  • Indigenous populations
  • Minority ethnic groups
  • LGBTQIA+ communities
  • Other underrepresented populations

Reasonable accommodation is available for applicants with disabilities during the recruitment process.

Why This Internship Stands Out

The Youth, Peace and Security Internship at the United Nations Population Fund offers a unique opportunity to contribute to regional peacebuilding initiatives while gaining practical experience within the United Nations system.

Participants will work directly on issues affecting youth participation in peace and security, gain exposure to international development programming, strengthen their research and communication skills, and build valuable professional networks that can support future careers in international development, humanitarian action, peacebuilding, and public policy.

For aspiring professionals seeking meaningful experience within a leading United Nations agency, this internship represents an outstanding pathway into international development and peacebuilding careers.

Application Deadline

19 June 2026

CLICK HERE TO APPLY

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global internship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post United Nations Population Fund Youth, Peace and Security Internship 2026 in Dakar, Senegal (Stipends are available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026: Funding Opportunity for Intercultural Exchange and Global Citizenship Projects Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Organizations working to promote intercultural understanding, global citizenship, international collaboration, and meaningful cross-cultural experiences now have an exciting opportunity to secure funding through the Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026. Lisle International has officially announced the opening of its Global Seed Grant application process, providing support for innovative projects that align with its mission of […]

The post Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026: Funding Opportunity for Intercultural Exchange and Global Citizenship Projects Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to promote intercultural understanding, global citizenship, international collaboration, and meaningful cross-cultural experiences now have an exciting opportunity to secure funding through the Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026.

Lisle International has officially announced the opening of its Global Seed Grant application process, providing support for innovative projects that align with its mission of building world citizens through intercultural engagement and international understanding.

The Global Seed Grant program is designed to help organizations launch, strengthen, and expand initiatives that foster meaningful connections across cultures, communities, and countries. Through a structured two-phase application process, selected organizations can access funding and personalized mentorship to help transform promising ideas into impactful projects.

Organizations interested in applying should prepare early, as the Request to Apply (RTA) submission window may close before the official deadline if a high volume of applications is received.

About Lisle International

Lisle International is dedicated to supporting a global community of changemakers by promoting intercultural experiences, international collaboration, and global citizenship.

Through its grant programs and international network, the organization helps individuals and institutions develop projects that bridge cultures, encourage understanding, and contribute to positive global engagement.

Its flagship Global Seed Grant program provides financial support and mentoring to organizations working to create transformative intercultural initiatives around the world.

What Are the Global Seed Grants?

The Global Seed Grants are competitive funding opportunities designed to support projects that align with Lisle International’s mission and values.

These grants aim to:

  • Promote intercultural understanding
  • Foster international collaboration
  • Encourage global citizenship
  • Support innovative educational initiatives
  • Build connections across diverse communities
  • Strengthen cross-cultural learning experiences
  • Empower organizations working for positive social impact

The program is particularly interested in supporting projects that demonstrate creativity, sustainability, and meaningful engagement among participants from different cultural backgrounds.

How the Grant Process Works

The Global Seed Grant program uses a two-phase application process.

This approach allows Lisle International to identify promising projects before inviting selected applicants to submit comprehensive proposals.

Phase One: Request to Apply (RTA)

The first step involves submitting a Request to Apply (RTA) through Lisle International’s online grant portal.

During this stage, applicants provide information about:

  • Their organization
  • Project concept
  • Expected impact
  • Alignment with Lisle International’s mission
  • Project goals and objectives

The RTA serves as an initial screening process.

Lisle reviews all submissions and identifies projects that demonstrate strong potential and alignment with the organization’s values.

Only applicants whose RTAs are approved will move forward to the second phase.

Why the RTA Matters

The Request to Apply is not merely an expression of interest.

It serves as a critical evaluation stage where reviewers assess:

  • Project relevance
  • Feasibility
  • Innovation
  • Potential impact
  • Organizational capacity
  • Alignment with grant priorities

Organizations should therefore invest time in preparing a strong and thoughtful RTA submission.

Phase Two: Full Proposal Submission

Organizations that successfully pass the RTA stage will receive an invitation to submit a full proposal.

This stage allows applicants to provide more detailed information about:

  • Project design
  • Implementation strategy
  • Expected outcomes
  • Budget
  • Monitoring and evaluation plans
  • Sustainability measures

The full proposal provides reviewers with a comprehensive understanding of the project’s objectives and potential impact.

Dedicated Grant Mentor Support

One of the most valuable features of the Lisle International Global Seed Grant program is the mentorship component.

Applicants invited to submit a full proposal are assigned a Lisle Grant Mentor.

What Does a Grant Mentor Do?

The mentor helps applicants:

  • Navigate the application process
  • Refine project concepts
  • Strengthen proposal quality
  • Align projects with Lisle’s mission and values
  • Clarify application requirements
  • Address questions and concerns

The mentor serves as a dedicated point of contact throughout the proposal development process.

This personalized support helps organizations present stronger and more competitive grant applications.

Funding Focus

While Lisle International emphasizes intercultural engagement and global citizenship, applicants should carefully review the eligibility criteria to ensure that their proposed projects align with the organization’s mission.

Projects may involve:

  • International education
  • Cross-cultural exchange
  • Global citizenship development
  • Youth engagement
  • Community partnerships
  • International collaboration
  • Cultural understanding initiatives
  • Educational exchanges
  • Leadership development
  • Social impact projects with intercultural components

Organizations are encouraged to explore Lisle’s eligibility requirements before beginning their applications.

Important Application Dates

Request to Apply (RTA) Opens

📅 5 June 2026

Request to Apply (RTA) Deadline

📅 30 June 2026

However, applicants should note that:

The RTA portal may close earlier than the advertised deadline if a high volume of submissions is received.

For this reason, organizations are strongly encouraged to submit as early as possible.

Notification of RTA Results

📅 Before the end of July 2026

Selected applicants will receive invitations to proceed to the full proposal stage.

Full Proposal Deadline

📅 1 September 2026

Only organizations invited after the RTA review process may submit full proposals.

Grant Recipient Announcement

📅 30 November 2026

Successful Global Seed Grant recipients will be selected during the annual Lisle Meeting and officially announced by the end of November.

Project Implementation Period

Funded projects are expected to be implemented during the following calendar year.

Project Timeline

📅 1 January – 31 December (Project Year)

Organizations should plan their activities within this implementation period.

Application Deadline Timing

Both the Request to Apply and the Full Proposal (for invited applicants) must be submitted no later than:

🕛 11:59 PM U.S. Eastern Time

Applicants should carefully account for time zone differences when planning their submissions.

Why Organizations Should Apply

The Lisle International Global Seed Grant offers several significant advantages beyond funding.

Financial Support

Selected organizations receive seed funding to launch or strengthen impactful projects.

Professional Mentorship

Applicants gain access to experienced grant mentors who provide valuable guidance throughout the process.

Global Network Access

Organizations become connected to a broader community of changemakers and intercultural practitioners.

Increased Visibility

Successful projects benefit from association with a respected international organization dedicated to global citizenship.

Capacity Building

The structured application and mentoring process helps organizations improve project planning and grant-writing skills.

Tips for a Strong Application

Organizations interested in applying should:

1. Demonstrate Clear Alignment

Show how the project directly supports intercultural understanding and global citizenship.

2. Focus on Impact

Clearly explain the difference the project will make and who will benefit.

3. Be Specific

Provide concrete objectives, activities, and outcomes rather than broad ideas.

4. Highlight Sustainability

Explain how project benefits will continue after the grant period.

5. Submit Early

Because the RTA portal may close before the official deadline, early submission is highly recommended.
